# audible-api-ts

> A fully typed TypeScript client for the Audible API — authentication, library, wishlist, catalog search.

## Install

```
bun add audible-api-ts
npm install audible-api-ts
```

Requires Node.js 18+ (native fetch and crypto).

## API

### login(locale: AudibleLocale): Promise<{ loginUrl, session, cookies }>

Generate a PKCE OAuth login URL for the given Audible marketplace.

```typescript
import { login } from 'audible-api-ts'
const { loginUrl, session, cookies } = await login('fr')
```

### register(authorizationCode: string, session: AuthSession): Promise<AudibleCredentials>

Exchange the OAuth authorization code for device credentials.

```typescript
import { register } from 'audible-api-ts'
const credentials = await register(code, session)
```

### refresh(credentials: AudibleCredentials): Promise<AudibleCredentials>

Manually refresh an access token. Usually not needed — all API functions (`library`, `wishlist`, `catalog`, `verify`) auto-refresh credentials before each call.

```typescript
import { refresh } from 'audible-api-ts'
const updated = await refresh(credentials)
```

### library(credentials: AudibleCredentials): Promise<{ items: AudibleItem[], credentials }>

Fetch the user's entire Audible library with automatic pagination. Credentials are auto-refreshed before the call.

```typescript
import { library } from 'audible-api-ts'
const { items } = await library(credentials)
items.map((book) => console.log(`${book.title} by ${book.authors.join(', ')}`))
```

### wishlist(credentials: AudibleCredentials): Promise<{ items: AudibleItem[], credentials }>

Fetch the user's entire Audible wishlist with automatic pagination. Credentials are auto-refreshed before the call.

```typescript
import { wishlist } from 'audible-api-ts'
const { items } = await wishlist(credentials)
```

### catalog(credentials: AudibleCredentials, options: CatalogOptions): Promise<{ items: AudibleItem[], credentials }>

Search the Audible catalog by category. Default sort is 'Relevance'. Credentials are auto-refreshed before the call.

**Sort behavior:**
- **API sorts (fast)** — single request, returns `limit` items: `'Relevance'` (default), `'BestSellers'`, `'AvgRating'`, `'ReleaseDate'`, `'-ReleaseDate'`, `'Title'`
- **Client sort (slow):** `'MostVoted'` — fetches ALL pages (up to 1000 items), sorts by votes desc then rating desc, returns `limit` items. Can take 10-60 seconds on large categories.

```typescript
import { catalog } from 'audible-api-ts'
const { items } = await catalog(credentials, {
  category: 'science-fiction',
  limit: 20,
})
```

### verify(credentials: AudibleCredentials): Promise<void>

Verify credentials are valid. Throws on invalid/expired credentials. Credentials are auto-refreshed before the call.

```typescript
import { verify } from 'audible-api-ts'
await verify(credentials)
```

## Types

### AudibleLocale

```
'fr' | 'com' | 'co.uk' | 'de' | 'it' | 'es' | 'ca' | 'com.au' | 'in' | 'co.jp'
```

### AudibleCredentials

```typescript
{
  accessToken: string
  refreshToken: string
  adpToken: string
  devicePrivateKey: string
  serial: string
  locale: AudibleLocale
  expiresAt: Date
}
```

### AudibleItem

Returned by library(), wishlist(), and catalog().

```typescript
{
  asin: string
  title: string
  subtitle?: string
  isbn?: string
  sku?: string
  authors: string[]
  narrators: string[]
  durationMinutes: number
  publisher?: string
  language?: string
  releaseDate?: Date
  coverUrl?: string
  summary?: string
  description?: string
  merchandisingSummary?: string
  copyright?: string
  formatType?: string
  contentType?: string
  contentDeliveryType?: string
  programParticipation?: string
  series?: { name: string; position?: number }
  categories: CategoryLadder[]
  keywords: string[]
  rating?: AudibleRating
  listeningStatus?: ListeningStatus
  purchaseDate?: Date
  dateAdded?: Date
  relationships: AudibleRelationship[]
  isAdultProduct: boolean
  productImages: Record<string, string>
  socialMediaImages: Record<string, string>
}
```

### AudibleRating

```typescript
{
  numReviews: number
  overallDistribution?: RatingDistribution
  performanceDistribution?: RatingDistribution
  storyDistribution?: RatingDistribution
}
```

### RatingDistribution

```typescript
{
  averageRating: number
  displayAverageRating: string
  displayStars: number
  numRatings: number
  numFiveStarRatings: number
  numFourStarRatings: number
  numThreeStarRatings: number
  numTwoStarRatings: number
  numOneStarRatings: number
}
```

### ListeningStatus

```typescript
{
  finishedAt?: Date
  isFinished?: boolean
  percentComplete?: number
  timeRemainingSeconds?: number
}
```

### CategoryLadder

```typescript
{
  root: string
  categories: { id: string; name: string }[]
}
```

### AudibleRelationship

```typescript
{
  asin: string
  relationshipToProduct: string
  relationshipType: string
  title?: string
  sort?: string
  url?: string
}
```

### CatalogOptions

```typescript
{
  category?: AudibleGenre       // genre name, resolved per locale
  categoryId?: string           // or raw Audible category ID
  sortBy?: 'MostVoted' | 'BestSellers' | 'AvgRating' | 'Relevance' | 'ReleaseDate' | '-ReleaseDate' | 'Title'
  limit?: number | 'all'       // max results to return (default 50)
  keywords?: string
  author?: string
  narrator?: string
}
```

### AudibleGenre

```
'science-fiction' | 'fantasy' | 'science-fiction-fantasy' | 'thriller' | 'mystery' | 'horror' | 'romance' | 'historical-fiction' | 'literary-fiction' | 'biography' | 'history' | 'business' | 'self-help' | 'science' | 'children' | 'young-adult' | 'comedy' | 'erotica' | 'religion' | 'sports' | 'travel' | 'lgbtq'
```

### AuthSession

```typescript
{
  codeVerifier: string
  serial: string
  locale: AudibleLocale
  createdAt: Date
}
```

### AudibleCookie

```typescript
{
  name: string
  value: string
  domain: string
}
```

## Constants

### AUDIBLE_LOCALES: Record<AudibleLocale, LocaleConfig>

Marketplace configurations. Example: `AUDIBLE_LOCALES['fr']` → `{ domain: 'fr', marketplaceId: 'A2728XDNODOQ8T', countryCode: 'fr' }`

### GENRE_CATEGORIES: Record<AudibleGenre, Partial<Record<AudibleLocale, string>>>

Genre-to-category-ID mapping per locale. Used internally by catalog() when `category` option is provided.

## Complete Example

```typescript
import { login, register, library, catalog } from 'audible-api-ts'

// 1. Auth
const { loginUrl, session, cookies } = await login('fr')
// → redirect user to loginUrl with cookies set
const credentials = await register(authorizationCode, session)

// 2. Library (credentials are auto-refreshed before API calls)
const { items } = await library(credentials)
items.map((book) => {
  const r = book.rating?.overallDistribution
  console.log(`${book.title} — ${r?.averageRating?.toFixed(1)}/5 (${r?.numRatings} votes)`)
  if (book.series) console.log(`  Series: ${book.series.name} #${book.series.position}`)
  if (book.listeningStatus?.percentComplete) console.log(`  Progress: ${book.listeningStatus.percentComplete}%`)
})

// 3. Catalog search
const { items: sf } = await catalog(credentials, {
  category: 'science-fiction',
  limit: 10,
})
sf.map((book, i) => console.log(`${i + 1}. ${book.title}`))
```
